text.title: Infrastructure as Code Basics Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules plus a skip-clause for `Infrastructure as Code Basics`. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Infrastructure MUST be declared declaratively (Terraform / Pulumi / CDK); imperative `gcloud`/`aws`-shell scripts in production are forbidden.

rationale.source: ThoughtWorks IaC essay; CNCF GitOps principles

rule.rationale: Declarative reconciliation produces idempotent diffs; imperative scripts hide state drift.

rule.statement: State MUST live in a remote, versioned backend (GCS / S3 / Azure Blob with object versioning); local state files are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Local state files lose on the first parallel apply.

rule.statement: Secret values MUST live outside state (Vault / Cloud Secret Manager) and be referenced by id; secrets in `*.tfvars` are forbidden in repos.
rule.rationale: Even encrypted state is plain JSON at rest in CI; committed `*.tfvars` leak through review tools.

rule.statement: Production `apply` MUST run from CI with WIF/OIDC; engineers must NOT have admin cloud credentials on workstations.
rule.rationale: Local applies bypass review, audit, and state locking.

rule.statement: Org-level baseline modules (network, IAM, audit-logs) MUST be vendored as versioned modules consumed by every project root.
rule.rationale: Without baseline modules, every project diverges; security drift accumulates.

rule.statement: If `Applies If` preconditions fail OR any `Skip If` trigger fires, do not run this methodology.
